unit.note: server vitest 172 files / 2005 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local). D21 adds 19 tests: preview-session-manager.validation.test.ts (6 AC2 behavioral regressions RED-first on pre-fix main: 5 fail / 1 pass) + preview-child-lifecycle.branches.test.ts (12: isValidPort x2, buildPreviewUrl x10 incl. host-drift/backslash/non-http-scheme/catch) + routes.test.ts (1: missing-port 400 mapping). Only 2 production files touched (preview-session-manager.ts -1 line ->392<=393; preview-child-lifecycle.ts +2 cohesive helpers). No client/** change. tsc + oxlint clean.

integration.note: 6 validation.test.ts regressions drive the REAL PreviewSessionManager.spawn()/doSpawn() through injected spawn/probe seams (F10 returned-URL host pinning: @evil.com/ -> http://localhost:5173/evil.com/, dashboard -> .../dashboard, absolute off-host -> bare origin, root -> host-only; F30 port reject: missing/0/-1/3000.5/70000 -> PreviewProfileInvalidError with spawn never called) + 1 route test pins PreviewProfileInvalidError -> 400 preview_profile_invalid. RED-first proven: 5/6 fail on pre-fix (the pass is the root case pre-fix concat already produced).

e2e.note: No preview E2E run. D21 is a server-side config-validation hardening with NO UI-observable change on a valid profile (the Preview button + /preview route + returned-URL shape for a normal ready_path are unchanged). The two defects are only reachable via a hand-authored malicious/incomplete profile; both are proven by deterministic server regressions + a 24-case adversarial boundary probe (15 URL smuggles + 9 ports, 0 off-host escapes) against the built dist. The client already handles preview_profile_invalid (PreviewButton.tsx); no client file changed.

surface_verification.justification: Server-side Preview config validation (FR-01.17); no UI-observable change on a valid profile. F10: the returned preview URL is now built via buildPreviewUrl (URL-constructor host+scheme-pinning against http://localhost:<port>, same normalization the readiness probe's buildReadyUrl uses) instead of raw string concatenation, so a malicious ready_path can't smuggle an off-host authority into window.open. F30: a missing/invalid dev_server.port is rejected up front with PreviewProfileInvalidError (route -> 400 preview_profile_invalid) instead of probing a dead port for 60s then killing the healthy dev server. Proof is the deterministic server regressions (validation.test.ts + branches.test.ts + routes.test.ts) green on this win32 host, plus the adversarial URL/port boundary probe (0 findings). No client/** file changed; the frontend-change detector only false-positives on the 'src/' substring inside 'server/src/'.

behaviors.behavior: AC1/F10 (MEDIUM) - the returned preview URL is host-pinned: a ready_path smuggling an authority ('@evil.com/') stays on-host as a path, not a new host
behaviors.evidence: validation.test.ts 'keeps a smuggled @evil.com/ ready_path on-host' - RED on pre-fix (http://localhost:5173@evil.com/, host evil.com) -> GREEN (http://localhost:5173/evil.com/, host localhost). branches.test.ts buildPreviewUrl x10 cover //, backslash, absolute off-host, javascript:/data:, catch.

behaviors.behavior: AC1/F10 (MEDIUM) - a slash-less ready_path yields an openable URL, and a root ready_path preserves the historical host-only shape (no trailing slash)
behaviors.evidence: validation.test.ts 'normalizes a slash-less ready_path' (pre-fix http://localhost:5173dashboard -> http://localhost:5173/dashboard) + 'still renders host-only for a root ready_path' (http://localhost:5173) - the root case pins the pre-existing contract so no literal-comparison regression.

behaviors.behavior: AC1/F30 (LOW) - a profile with dev_server.command but a missing/invalid port is rejected before spawning (never probes port 0 / never kills the healthy dev server)
behaviors.evidence: validation.test.ts 'rejects a profile whose dev_server.port is missing - never spawns' + 'rejects a zero / negative / non-integer / out-of-range port' - RED on pre-fix (port defaulted to 0, probe skipped, spawn resolved) -> GREEN (PreviewProfileInvalidError, spawn never called). branches.test.ts isValidPort x2.

behaviors.behavior: AC1/F30 (LOW) - the route maps the missing-port PreviewProfileInvalidError to 400 preview_profile_invalid (config-omission code, not a 5xx spawn error)
behaviors.evidence: routes.test.ts '400 maps a missing-port PreviewProfileInvalidError -> preview_profile_invalid' asserts status 400 + detail 'dev_server.port must be a positive integer'.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression test is RED on pre-fix main and green after (defect actually reproduced)
behaviors.evidence: validation.test.ts run against unmodified source: 5 failed / 1 passed (the 4 URL smuggle/normalize cases + the 2 port-reject cases fail; root case passes). After the fix: 6/6 green.

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full existing server suite stays green; no invariant/ADR regressed (D03 shell:false/win32 spawn, D20 lifecycle, ADR-044 single-spawn-path preserved)
behaviors.evidence: 172 files / 2005 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local). preview-child-lifecycle.test.ts (D20) + preview-win32 guards still green; tsc + oxlint clean.

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within footprint (helpers in the cohesive child-lifecycle module + a new test file per the campaign guardrail) and bloat baselines are not ratcheted
behaviors.evidence: preview-session-manager.ts 392<=393 (net -1), preview-session-manager.test.ts 341<=341 (untouched), preview-child-lifecycle.ts 267<300, branches.test.ts 290<300, validation.test.ts 153<300, routes.test.ts 280<300. Anti-ratchet hook clean.

behaviors.behavior: Confidence calibration (touches_io_boundary) - adversarial URL/port boundary probe: no ready_path resolves off-host, no invalid port validates
behaviors.evidence: Node probe over built dist: 15 adversarial ready_paths (@/////\\/absolute/https/javascript/data/user:pass@/leading-space/query/fragment) all resolve host=localhost (0 off-host); 9 ports incl. '3000'(string)/undefined/NaN/65536 all validate as expected (0 mis-validations).

test_completeness.note: 4 ACs (AC1-AC4) split across F10 (2) + F30 (2) + AC2 RED-first + AC3 + AC4 + the io-boundary calibration probe, all tested. Enumeration basis = the sub-iterate spec's Acceptance Criteria; each maps to >=1 RED-first regression in preview-session-manager.validation.test.ts (findings F10/F30) plus per-branch unit coverage in preview-child-lifecycle.branches.test.ts.
